title: malvaviscus arboreus var. drummondii
intro: Malvaviscus arboreus var. drummondii, also known as the firecracker flower or sabadilla, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and alleviating symptoms of fever, cough, and bronchitis. Malvaviscus arboreus var. drummondii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice, and its roots and leaves are also used in herbal medicine to create salves and ointments.
